rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the scope of the annex.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Component type contracts should use visible feature names and constants for assumptions and guarantees.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
